examples: Why doesn't the president say so without equivocation?
They must admit without equivocation or ambiguity that the organization is the sole and only representative of the Palestinian people.
He looks at me sharply, distrusting my equivocation.
It is a bare knuckle affair full of palter and equivocation from all parties.
Not surprisingly, the Berlusconi government has reacted to the Russian invasion of Georgia with moral equivocation.
